scenarios.evidence: User confirmed extended display streaming works end-to-end; Mac log: 'capture started: 2816x1940 display 17 mode extend backend=CGDisplayStream-EDR localCursor=true fps=120 hdr=true'; device log: 'status: Receiving 2816x1940 (ProRes)'.
User confirmed HDR pass; Mac log negotiated 'hdr=true' with EDR capture backend and status 'Extending to iPad (2816x1940 @120Hz HDR)'.
User confirmed 120fps pass; Mac log 'fps=120' with virtual display created at 120Hz; device announced maxFps=120.
User confirmed audio has no issues; device log 'audio playback started (48000Hz stereo)' with Mac system audio tap live at 48000Hz.

Initially failed: the Mac ignored mid-session rotation hellos on the CGDisplayStream-EDR backend (guard checked only the SCK stream). Fixed in opendisplay commit 3e21563, then verified live both directions: Mac log 01:17:11 'reconfiguring for 1940x2816' -> 'virtual display created: 970x1408pt' -> 'capture started: 1940x2816', and 01:17:14 back to 2816x1940.

User confirmed touch input has no issues (touch phases injected on the Mac). Perceived mouse-cursor lag was diagnosed as implicit CALayer animation on the 120Hz local-cursor overlay, fixed in photonport-ios commit f648668, and user confirmed the delay is gone.
User confirmed disconnect/reconnect pass; logs show repeated session teardown ('session v3 ended: peer_closed') and rebinding with fresh generations, stream resuming each time.

User confirmed physical cable unplug/replug recovers with no issues; Mac log shows 'usb connection failed ... Connection lost - retrying' followed by automatic redial, session v3 re-accept, and capture resume.

SAS numeric-comparison pairing performed by the user on this physical pair: device log 03:55:14 'pairing: advertising _photonport._tcp (pair=1)', 03:55:17 'pairing: SAS ready for LTG Mac Studio', 03:55:20 'pairing: user confirmed SAS - storing key'. The resulting TLS-PSK credential authenticated every subsequent WiFi session.

User streamed interactively over WiFi (changed quality settings, observed latency): Mac log repeatedly shows 'TCP transport secured with TLS-PSK (paired)' followed by session v3 accept and HEVC-Main10-HLG streaming (generations 4-10); device log 'status: Receiving 1688x1164'. WiFi rotation also verified (portrait/landscape virtual display rebuilds at 01:43-01:44).

Observed live 2026-07-22 21:52-21:57 KST: the user revoked the Mac's pairing on the device (Settings > Paired Macs swipe-delete). The active USB session ended immediately and every subsequent USB preface was refused (socket closed). An unpaired Wi-Fi connect attempt then failed structurally at the TLS-PSK handshake for 55+ seconds with no session and no pre-authentication oracle from the receiver. Fresh SAS numeric-comparison re-pairing (user confirmed matching digits on both ends) restored connectivity: session v3 accepted (generation 39) over TLS within one second of confirmation.

Observed live 2026-07-22 21:36-21:39 KST on the authenticated wire: with the USB primary streaming (session v3 generation 37, ProRes), a competing paired Wi-Fi claim from a second sender instance was rejected by the receiver with session_busy 16 consecutive times at the 5s retry cadence; Mac UI showed 'Receiver is in use by another Mac' and the USB primary streamed without interruption (no preemption). Complementary case: after the user explicitly disconnected the Wi-Fi primary, a subsequent USB claim was accepted normally (generation 37), matching the explicit-release rule.

Ten-plus observed WiFi reconnect cycles (quality switches, app restarts, user disconnects): each performed a fresh TLS-PSK handshake, session v3 rebind with new generation, and stream resume within ~0.3-3.5s, including 'static screen after reconnect - replaying last frame as keyframe'.
